pi_items.source: Task specification requires creating table named 'GalaInvitees' with columns CustomerId, FullName, Email, TotalSpent

Queried Chinook SQLite database (official GitHub version https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database). Query: SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName as FullName, c.Email, SUM(i.Total) as TotalSpent FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 15; Returns exactly 15 rows.

Downloaded official Chinook database from GitHub and verified schema matches expected structure (tables: Customer, Invoice, Employee, Album, Artist, Genre, MediaType, Track, Playlist, PlaylistTrack). The path /opt/libreoffice_base_samples/Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite is referenced in export_result.sh and create_chinook_odb.py scripts as the expected deployment location.

pi_items.source: Verified by querying official Chinook SQLite database from GitHub with query: SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName, c.Email, SUM(i.Total) FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 15

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Chinook database, a well-known public sample database created by Luis Rocha representing a digital media store with customers, invoices, employees, and music catalog data. The data is real (not synthetic) and sourced from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database. The task requires identifying the top 15 customers by total spending (lifetime invoice total) and populating them into a new 'GalaInvitees' table. The correct top 15 customers by spending are: Helena Holý ($49.62), Richard Cunningham ($47.62), Luis Rojas ($46.62), Ladislav Kovács ($45.62), Hugh O'Reilly ($45.62), Frank Ralston ($43.62), Julia Barnett ($43.62), Fynn Zimmermann ($43.62), Astrid Gruber ($42.62), Victor Stevens ($42.62), Terhi Hämäläinen ($41.62), František Wichterlová ($40.62), Isabelle Mercier ($40.62), Johannes Van der Berg ($40.62), and Luís Gonçalves ($39.62). The database has 59 total customers, making 15 a reasonable selection threshold.
